directive.title: Mobile Responsive — Charts and Data Visualizations

auditor.summary: The generated code creates the ChartWrapper component and useChartDimensions hook as specified, with correct CSS overrides for mobile responsiveness. However, there are several critical issues: (1) No page-level modifications were made to actually use ChartWrapper on the 5 dashboard pages, making the acceptance criteria unverifiable. (2) The component detection logic for identifying Recharts sub-components (XAxis, YAxis, Legend, Tooltip) is fragile and unreliable, relying on prop presence rather than component type detection. (3) No tests were provided despite the acceptance criteria requiring Jest and Playwright verification. (4) The index.ts exports may cause TypeScript compilation errors. (5) No Lighthouse CI configuration was implemented. The build is CONDITIONAL because the core infrastructure is correct but significant functional gaps exist that prevent meeting the acceptance criteria. The code needs page-level integration, more robust component detection, and comprehensive testing before it can be considered complete.

top_issues.description: Component detection logic is fragile and unreliable. The code attempts to identify Recharts components (XAxis, YAxis, Legend, Tooltip) by checking for specific props (dataKey, type, payload, iconType, cursor, labelFormatter) or displayName. This approach will fail for many valid Recharts configurations. For example, XAxis without dataKey prop, custom Legend components, or Tooltip with different prop combinations. This is a CRITICAL functional issue that will cause the wrapper to silently fail to apply mobile overrides.

The enhanceChild function recursively processes children but does not handle the case where children is a single ReactElement vs an array consistently. The initial call passes a single ReactElement (children prop), but inside the recursion it checks for Array.isArray. This works but is fragile. More critically, the function does not handle Fragment children or multiple top-level chart components.

The LegendProps interface is defined locally but the index.ts also exports LegendProps from 'recharts'. This creates a naming conflict. The local interface may not match Recharts' actual LegendProps type, causing type mismatches.
